prediction.chief_complaint: left big toe joint pain, swelling, erythema (suspected gout flare)

medications.name: indomethacin
medications.frequency: three times daily with food until flare resolves (5–7 days)

medications.name: allopurinol
medications.frequency: once daily, to start after flare settles and titrate up

diagnoses.description: acute gout flare, left first metatarsophalangeal joint

plan: indomethacin 50 mg three times daily with food for 5–7 days until flare resolves
ice the left first MTP joint, elevate, rest
start allopurinol 100 mg once daily after flare settles; titrate up to goal
maintain uric acid target under 6 mg/dL
dietary modification: limit red meat, shellfish, beer

follow_up.reason: follow-up for gout flare resolution and uric acid management
